behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: summary-write path must query/read persisted per-dimension findings for the same round and enforce a block. Unit is too low because a pure function would miss repo/query wiring mistakes (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA no), no model-output dependence (LLM-DEP no), and no multi-agent sequence (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected higher levels because behavior text is strictly about stored verdict consistency, not live infra or LLM decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary service + findings repository (test DB). Seed same-round findings including one FAIL, attempt to persist PASS summary, assert write is rejected and rejection reason references conflicting stored finding.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears when gate computation and persistence layer interact: gate must source verdicts from stored findings, not summary text. A unit test of computation logic alone can miss incorrect data-source wiring. No real infra dependency, no AI output dependency, no multi-step agent chain. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is data-source selection in local components.
behaviors.test_description: Set stored findings to include FAIL while summary text claims all PASS. Run stop-gate computation through service + repository stack and assert final verdict is non-PASS based on stored findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: This is policy enforcement across input handling + persistence/audit logging. Unit is insufficient because missing audit-write or flag plumbing is an inter-module failure. No requirement for real OS/network/subprocess, no model-quality dependence, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected higher levels since behavior text only requires explicit flag and warning record creation.
behaviors.test_description: Through API/service layer, submit manual override without flag and assert rejection; submit with flag and assert summary accepted plus audit warning record persisted with actor, round, and timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is temporal/state consistency between commit metadata and review-round metadata across modules. Integration catches mismatched lookup/comparison logic. Behavior text does not require real git subprocess/network; commit and round identifiers can be represented in-process/test DB. No model-output quality dependence and no mandatory multi-agent chain in this specific check. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow due lack of explicit real infra or LLM-decision requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed fix commit marker and review rounds where latest round predates fix commit; assert PASS finalization blocked. Seed a post-fix round identifier; assert gate allows PASS eligibility.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets live loop sequencing with agent execution after updated diff. Real failure is in end-to-end orchestration: update diff -> run review agent round -> finalize gate. Integration stubs can falsely pass by simulating completion without proving live loop sequencing (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic alone is close, but behavior is not just one model call; it requires ordered process steps in the loop (MULTI-STEP yes). Rejected lower levels because text requires a fresh round in the live workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end multi-step review flow on a changed diff: attempt PASS before rerun (must fail), trigger real review-agent round on updated diff, then re-evaluate finalization gate (eligible only after that new round id exists).

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic gate policy over persisted findings + round counters across modules; integration needed to catch state read/aggregation bugs. No real OS/network/subprocess required, no dependence on model output content, and no mandatory multi-agent sequence in the behavior itself. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is about stored unresolved MUST-FIX findings at exhaustion.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored results with unresolved MUST-FIX and mark round budget exhausted. Execute gate outcome computation through service+repo and assert escalation status, with PASS explicitly disallowed.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live multi-round workflow and exhaustion handling, so minimum boundary is full sequence validation across rounds. Integration can miss orchestration bugs where round transitions/finalization paths differ in runtime loop. Agentic is involved but insufficient alone because correctness depends on chained stages across multiple rounds (MULTI-STEP yes). Rejected lower levels due explicit 'live multi-round review workflow' wording.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live multi-round loop until configured round limit with unresolved MUST-FIX retained in stored findings; assert final state blocks PASS and emits escalation event/record at exhaustion.
